desc:
The forest here is a tangled mess of thorns and brush, you can probably move
east or west, but traversing the forest in the northern or southernly directions
would be impossible. On the brighter side, a few small animals can be seen in
the area, making the forest a tad less sorrowful.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
The canopy overhead has begun to thin, letting through enough light to
distinguish night from day, but not yet enough to permit a vigorous stand of
underbrush. Northwards however, a large amount of brush has blocked travel, and
southwards a robust group of vines does the same.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
Small amounts of underbrush can be seen to the north, suggesting more light than
is usual for this portion of the forest. In all other directions the canopy
overhead has kept the ground clear of vegetation, albeit a bit dark also.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
The forest here overlooks a small trail to the south, and fends off a field to
the west. Underbrush is a common obstacle, but easily routed around. The canopy
overhead is almost complete, providing just a little light to the plants that
live closer to the earth.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
As you travel under the grace of the trees you are reminded of your small
stature in comparison with the rest of the world. Be you a legend among men you
are still but an ant among the more fearsome creatures of the forest.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
Several small stands of mushrooms can be found along the forest floor in this
area. Sprouting out from the matt of needles that form the forest floor they
provide the housekeeping for the forest, decomposing the more protein rich
substances that find their way here.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
This forest must be centuries old, its large trees raising towards the sky, each
in its own space, meshing perfectly above with its neighbours. Gone however, are
the protectors of the forest, as is evident by the various forms of desecration
practiced against their woody skin.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
A sense of evil pervades the forest here, as if something powerful and sinister
was watching your every move. A few ravens can be heard screeching overhead, but
the sky has been covered with a leafy replacement that readily conceals the
birds.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
The buzz of insects can be heard, coming from a large bush near the southern
edge of the forest. Closer inspection reveals a dead raccoon, beset upon by a
horde of flies and be The insects have finished off too much of it to make any
judgement on how it died.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
Large amounts of underbrush fill the space in-between the trees here, where the
grass nearby fights for ground against the tall trees of the forest. It is
questionable who will win this war, for both sides - the grass and the trees -
look tired and unhealthy.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
A huge blackberry bush sits on the western edge of the forest here, separating
the forest from a clearing to the west. It is certainly possible to manuever
around the bush, and a few small animals can be seen using it as shelter.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
Like so many pillars holding up the sky, the trees of this forest have created a
vast hall. With the canopy overhead providing shelter from the light and heat of
day, and some shelter from the rains and winds, the only thing missing is
dinner. Of course, that may be your purpose here.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
A solid stand of trees blocks the way west. Hidden among them, and surely
unreachable by you, one can just barely glimpse the large nests of some giant
birds hidden at the base of the canopy. You cannot determine what kind of bird
it is that dwells in the darkness under the canopy, but it surely must have good
night-vision to feed its young.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
Thick vegetation and spiny creepers choke off any thoughts of passage to the
west or north here. The trees continue to grow thick and support their
tremendous canopy, but you can see faint hints of light northwards, where the
forest may be thinning.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
The barren forest floor has been rudely interupted by large amounts of brush and
creepers to the north and east, making those directions impassable. A few small
animals can be seen scurrying around the vegetation, but none can be seen
foraging any farther into the forest.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
Several large trees grow close together to the east, forming a natural barrier
for travellers. Numerous choke creepers can be seen winding their way around the
trees in this area, their thick thorns guarding the trunks of the trees.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
This grassy field separates the forest to the north from a small trail to the
south. The grass itself is more a mixture of various weeds, all quite happy and
healthy. A couple cows, or a herd of sheep would survive well here.
name: Withering Grass

desc:
This large field of grass, broken by the occasional lone tree, extends north and
east until it meets the border of a large forest, and south until stopped by a
trail that is situated between it and the southern forest.
name: Withering Grass

desc:
The forest seems to have been split and pushed open here, with a massive
collection of trees smashed to the sides, forming a natural barricade between
the clearing and the rest of the forest. A few saplings have begun to return the
area to the clutches of the forest, thus providing evidence that whatever trauma
did this has not been back.
name: Trampled Ground

desc:
The forest here has been decimated. Many hundreds of logs have been uprooted and
pushed aside, and the ground bears large ditches, as if two gigantic beasts had
once fought here. Whatever it might have been seems to be gone now, as one can
see the small plants and saplings that have begun to grow again.
name: Trampled Ground

desc:
You have entered a small den, inhabited by two large wargs. The ceilings are low
and the space cramped. In the centre of the room a large pile of furs is being
used as bedding, undoubtably from the warg's previous meals. The exit is easy to
notice from this side, a narrow opening just big enough to let the sun filter
inside during the end of the day. The entrance does take some effort to squeeze
through.
name: Small Nook

desc:
The tunnel grows narrow and cramped as it squeezes through the bedrock, trying,
apparently in vain, to ply its way east. To the west it opens out somewhat, and
the air in that direction seems fresher, though hung with an overtaste of decay.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
Cramped and dark, this tunnel would be enough to strike terror into the mind of
anyone with claustrophobia. The walls stand at a slight angle, leaning toward
one another at the top, as if falling in upon you, and the uneven and pitted
floor makes progress through the area quite perilsome. However, a soft but
steady breeze from the east seems to promise relief in that direction.
name: In a Low-roofed Tunnel

desc:
Before you is a branching of ways. In one direction a broad, though rough- hewn
tunnel cuts into the cold, hard rock. To the north, however, the digging seems
to have failed, for it appears that the tunnel does not proceed much further
that way. Lastly, you might choose to follow a narrow and low-roofed passageway
to the east, from whence a slight cool breeze drifts in.
name: A Crumbling Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel has become smaller but still shows signs of having been used
recently. The air is still foul and disgusting but the roof of the tunnel is
cracked and broken. Here and there soil and water seeps through and drops onto
the tunnel floor.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
Three tunnels meet here, branching off to the north, the east and the west. From
the west can be heard a faint sound of running water, whilst from the north a
slight yet perceptible breeze drifts in, carrying with it not only the promise
of fresh air, but also an undercurrent of decay.
name: At a Nexus

desc:
Here a rough spartan quarters has been burrowed into the rock. There is filth
and garbage everywhere. All around you the shattered possessions of the former
occupants lie in untidy heaps. A single tunnel exits this area, and it appears
that attempts to dig onward in the opposite direction met with failure, as the
roof has crumbled in at that end of the chamber.
name: At a Caved-in Tunnel

desc:
A little tunnel through the rocks has been cut out apparently for escape
purposes. You can see no exits but you sense a cold draft from somewhere. You
see some scratches on the walls.
name: An Escape Route

desc:
This room once served as the living and sleeping quarters of some kind. Now it
appears to be just a mess of shattered benches, broken stone shelves and
decaying piles of garbage.
name: A Crudely Carved Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is dark and disgusting. You feel nauseous from breathing the stale
and disgusting air. However the walls and room appear firm and trustworthy.
name: A Crudely Carved Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is dark and disgusting. You feel nauseous from breathing the stale
and disgusting air. However the walls and room appear firm and trustworthy.
name: A Crudely Carved Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is dark and disgusting. You feel nauseous from breathing the stale
and disgusting air. However the walls and room appear firm and trustworthy.
name: A Crudely Carved Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is dark and disgusting. You feel nauseous from breathing the stale
and disgusting air. However the walls and room appear firm and trustworthy.
name: A Crudely Carved Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is dark and disgusting. You feel nauseous from breathing the stale
and disgusting air. The walls and floor of the tunnel are riddled with large
cracks. Occasionally you hear the rock groan as it shifts. You hurry on fearing
a collapse.
name: A Crudely Carved Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is dark and disgusting. You feel nauseous from breathing the stale
and disgusting air. The walls and floor of the tunnel are riddled with large
cracks. Occasionally you hear the rock groan as it shifts. You hurry on fearing
a collapse.
name: A Crudely Carved Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is dark and disgusting. You feel nauseous from breathing the stale
and disgusting air. The walls and floor of the tunnel are riddled with large
cracks. Occasionally you hear the rock groan as it shifts. You hurry on fearing
a collapse.
name: A Crudely Carved Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is dark and disgusting. You feel nauseous from breathing the stale
and disgusting air. However the walls and room appear firm and trustworthy.
name: A Crudely Carved Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is dark and disgusting. You feel nauseous from breathing the stale
and disgusting air. However the walls and room appear firm and trustworthy.
name: A Crudely Carved Tunnel

desc:
Several tunnels meet here and above you a rough stone staircase makes its way up
through the rock. The steps of the stairs are strewn with rubble and old bones.
name: At a Nexus

desc:
This tunnel comes abruptly to a stop here, due to the presence of tons of fallen
rocks and dirt. Luckily you weren't in its way when it fell, otherwise you would
simply be suffocated under the immense weight of the debris. The obvious exit is
south, as the way up is impossible.
name: Caved-in Tunnel

desc:
A great and recent calamity has occurred here. Vast sections of the tunnel walls
have collapsed blocking the passageway. There appears to be no way through.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is dark and disgusting. You feel nauseous from breathing the stale
and disgusting air. However the walls and room appear firm and trustworthy.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is dark and disgusting. You feel nauseous from breathing the stale
and disgusting air. However the walls and room appear firm and trustworthy.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
You have entered a dark tunnel. The air is stale and smells disgusting. The
walls of the tunnel are shattered and broken and you fear that the roof itself
might collapse upon you.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
You have entered a dark tunnel. The air is stale and smells disgusting. The
walls of the tunnel are shattered and broken and you fear that the roof itself
might collapse upon you.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is dark and disgusting. You feel nauseous from breathing the stale
and disgusting air. However the walls and room appear firm and trustworthy.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
The dark and disgusting tunnel is filled with rancid air. However, the walls
look firm and the ground strong enough to support any creature that might tread
upon them.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
Here a stream cuts its way across the tunnel floor. You can sense rather than
see the points of entry and exit. No light seeps in from outside but the stream
moves swift and strong as if it abhors this place and strives to leave as fast
as it can.
name: An Underground Stream

desc:
The tunnel suddenly grows smaller and more oppressive. You find yourself having
to squeeze by some narrow places and bend your head beneath the low hanging
roof. The air is full of strange currents and odours.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel suddenly grows smaller and more oppressive. You find yourself having
to squeeze by some narrow places and bend your head beneath the low hanging
roof. The air is full of strange currents and odours.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel suddenly grows smaller and more oppressive. You find yourself having
to squeeze by some narrow places and bend your head beneath the low hanging
roof. The air is full of strange currents and odours.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
You are in a tunnel that seems old and unfinished. The walls are in places
smooth and in other places rough. It is as if a work long ago begun was
completed by other less skilled hands. The air here stinks and you feel
reluctant to proceed.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
This peculiar side passage seems to burrow indiscriminately through the thick
dirt itself. Only creatures of massive strength could attempt such a feat, but
the lack of any refinements indicates a lack for the finer points of
construction. The smell of filth and loam fills the air.
name: A Gutted Tunnel

desc:
Onwards, the tunnel continues, a gaping maw carved in the very ground of Arda.
Roots stick out from all directions, as well as the blind eyes of very large
earthworms. The thick rancid smell of troll pervades the tunnel, leaving no
doubts as to who carved this path.
name: A Gutted Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel is actually quite large for one beneath the ground. The thick dirt
and debris indicate you are not far from the surface, yet you feel closed in by
the dark, uneven surfaces.
name: A Gutted Tunnel

desc:
Pieces of dirt and rock filter down from the ceiling, covering your head or
helmet in its filth. This does nothing to assuage your fears about the stability
of this tunnel, yet it seems to have gotten a lot of use. Large, splayed
footprints lead both ways, and a rancid, metallic smell dominates your senses.
name: A Gutted Tunnel

desc:
This section of the tunnel ends abruptly into a smooth wall of stone. The tunnel
has many rough sections along the ceiling and walls, and the air is heavy with
the smell of some foul odor. Along the northern wall, there is a huge rock which
might be moved with great strength.
name: Stone Tunnel

desc:
Bending sharply to the east and south, the tunnel continues under the immense
forest above. You are not sure which is safer, the daunting growth above or the
lurking dangers here. One thing is sure, safety is no where close. The stench of
troll keeps your mind focused on any possible threats.
name: Earthy Tunnel

desc:
Running north and south, the earthern passageway burrows along at an even angle.
Tiny bits of dirt fall on your head from the unsupported ceiling, but the roots
seem to be holding it together enough to prevent a collapse. The smell of troll
is quite strong here.
name: Earthy Tunnel

desc:
This is the beginning of the earth troll's tunnels. Dug crudely but efficiently
from the very ground, this passage is damp from the wet soil all around you.
Twigs, decaying branches, crumbling leaves, and roots all form the walls, floor,
and ceiling. A thick, musty smell pervades the air, making breathing a little
difficult. Your senses stay alert to the danger that lies down these parts.
name: Earthy Tunnel

desc:
Worming your way down through the narrow dirt passage, you end up in a
surprisingly large cavern. Crude wooden supports hold up a massive ceiling of
hard dirt and roots. Various items from kills lies scattered about-- bones, bits
of clothing, pieces of equipment. The smell of troll droppings is enough by
itself to warrant your departure, but the inhabitants have other ideas.
name: Lair of the Earth Trolls

desc:
After worming your way through the hidden passage, you find yourself in an even
smaller passage. A strange hole lies in the corner of this small area, beckoning
you with the promise of...something. The smell of troll here is almost
overwhelming.
name: Hidden Burrow

desc:
You are on a rough hewn staircase that is cut through the living rock. There is
no artistry here though, the steps are well worn but still the rough manufacture
is evident. To one side you see a gaping hole in the wall as if some mighty
force has broken through. The shattered remnants of a doorframe are still in
evidence. To the other side someone has turned a large step into a kitchen by
breaking a small cavern out of the rock. A large cauldron bubbles, and a light
yellow smoke rises and turns the ceiling yellow and black. Crude shelves cover
the walls, all kinds of things crammed into them. The floor is covered in a
thick layer of some goo that has squirted out of the cauldron over time.
name: A Stone Stair

desc:
Here a rough spartan quarters has been burrowed into the rock. There is filth
and garbage everywhere. All around you the shattered possessions of the former
occupants lie in untidy heaps. The dark stains are probably blood, you wonder
what mighty calamity occurred here.
name: A Deserted Cave

desc:
The tunnel has become smaller but still shows signs of having been used
recently. The air is still foul and disgusting but the roof of the tunnel is
cracked and broken. Here and there soil and water seeps through and drops onto
the tunnel floor.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel has become smaller but still shows signs of having been used
recently. The air is still foul and disgusting but the roof of the tunnel is
cracked and broken. Here and there soil and water seeps through and drops onto
the tunnel floor.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel has become smaller but still shows signs of having been used
recently. The air is still foul and disgusting but the roof of the tunnel is
cracked and broken. Here and there soil and water seeps through and drops onto
the tunnel floor.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
The trolls' network of filthy and crude passages branches in three directions
here. A large number of tracks lead to the west, indicating that something
important to them lies in that direction.
name: Rough Junction

desc:
This immense circular arena serves as an important meeting place for the troll
race. The crudely carved walls lend silent emphasis to the immense strength
which dominates the trolls' way of life. Simple, yet vital, troll functions take
place here, as they challenge each other and practice skills which help them
survive in the harsh sunlit lands.
name: Troll Arena

desc:
Pieces of discarded armour and broken weaponry scatter the floor of this cave.
The centre area has been cleared, as the bare ground only houses a layer of dirt
and dried-up blood. Many weapon-inflicted cracks are visible in the cave's
walls, marking the cave as a place of battle and not a garbage pile. A crude
flight of stone stairs in the northwestern corner of the cave leads downwards
through a large opening.
name: Troll Crusher Cave

desc:
Bits of broken weapons, armour, and other assorted pieces of equipment lie
scattered all over this room. Maybe something useful has been left by the other
trolls, but usually junk really is just junk.
name: Refuse Room

desc:
The tunnel leads onward into darkness, exits only to the north and south. The
sound of dripping water comes from all directions making it impossible for you
to hear if anything lies ahead.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel extends north and south here, but absolute blackness conceals any
clues to what lies in either direction. The dripping sound is incredibly loud
here!! You have no idea what could cause this racket, it can't possibly be due
to just echoes from cave walls. In any event, you doubt that you could shout
above the din as it is almost impossibly loud.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
Refuse is carelessly piled here, and judging by the matted straw in one corner
something uses this place to sleep. Small biting flies swarm from pieces of
rotting meat whenever the garbage is disturbed. There doesn't appear to be any
exit from this chamber save the intersection to the west.
name: Garbage Pile

desc:
The areas to the east and south are choked with rotting debris - mostly old
carcasses that have not quite finished decomposing. To the west, the passage is
not as cluttered, and it seems to be slowly ascending.
name: Intersection

desc:
The passage becomes almost vertical here - leading slightly north as it goes up.
Apparently this tunnel was built to take advantage of a natural fissure in the
rock, for the walls have a smooth look not common below. The surface must be
nearing - the tunnel above looks as if it is lined with hard earth.
name: Bottom of a Natural Chimney

desc:
The passage bends from east to north here, rising sharply as it does so. This
tunnel is an example of the crude techniques used by trolls when constructing
living areas away from the sun - the walls are jagged, and in many places it
looks as if the rock was shattered by brute force and carted away.
name: Rocky Passageway

desc:
The tunnel leads onward into the darkness, the only obvious exits to the north
and to the south. The sound of dripping water comes from every direction, making
it impossible to hear if anything lies ahead.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
You come to a crossroads of sorts: three exits leading off to the west, south,
and north. It looks as if this intersection was created by accident, pure
coincidence that the trolls decided to delve in those directions. Perhaps there
was some reasoning behind their choices, especially if their survival was at
stake.
name: Clumsy Intersection

desc:
You enter into a tunnel leading both east and west. It seems equally dark and
silent in each direction, except for the omnipresent sound of dripping water. A
cold chill runs through you as you realise that you could remain lost down here
for many, many days.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
A dark and narrow tunnel winds through the stone. Water occasionally trickles in
through very small cracks creating little drops of moisture, which glitter in
the dark. The tunnel twists out of sight to the east behind a wall of dark rock.
To the north, it broadens and descends slightly.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel is littered with remnants of carcasses in various states of
decomposition. Splintered bones that have been gnawed and drained of marrow
crunch under each footstep, while oozing maggots digest whatever morsels of meat
that have remained undevoured. To the north, some sort of inter- section can be
made out in the gloom.
name: Trash-filled Tunnel

desc:
While checking the walls, you realize that the once compact and rich dirt is now
quite soggy. Your mouth waters in anticipation of a potential drink of fresh
water in this unhealthy place.
name: A Damp Tunnel

desc:
You emerge from the tunnel into a small, cleared-out cavern. Scraps of meat and
entrails lie on the floor here, giving the room a nasty smell.
name: A Dark Cavern

desc:
These tunnels must be near the surface, for the walls are of packed earth and a
few intrepid tree roots dangle from the ceiling. The floor of the passage is
bedrock, and the tunnel dives down to the south through a fissure in the rocks.
To the east, this tunnel continues.
name: Bend in the Passage

desc:
Water drips from a chamber overhead, dampening the mixture of packed earth and
stone that makes up the floor. Heavy footprints are clearly visible leading to
the east. Crude steps have been hewn to aid in clambering up to the chamber
above.
name: Damp Intersection

desc:
A rockfall dominates the eastern half of this chamber. Large slabs of rock and
copious amounts of dirt have fallen from the ceiling, leaving a pile of rubble
below and a cavernous expanse above. The rockfall appears to have broken through
into an existing passage below; two particularly large slabs of stone have
become wedged above this opening, creating an impromptu archway and keeping the
opening from becoming obstructed. In the western half of the chamber, away from
the rockfall, a number of crude beds of leaves and scattered bones indicate that
this chamber is used as a sleeping area for trolls.
name: Partially-collapsed Chamber

desc:
This chamber of packed earth has its walls strengthened by many tree roots.
Apparently this must be near the surface, but no hint of fresh air can be felt.
Water is leaking from the ceiling, and the droplets that hit the floor coalesce
into small rivulets that drain through the hole in the floor. There are some
crude steps cut into the wall to help one to climb out of here.
name: Upper Chamber

desc:
You are inside a cave that lies concealed in the ground. From the dimensions of
the cave and the skulls and rotting flesh here, you deduce that this is the
dwelling of some large carnivorous creatures.
name: A Deep Hole

desc:
As you enter this narrow tunnel, your shoulders barely brush the walls on either
side of you. The tunnel continues straight ahead into the darkness, and it seems
to have a slight downward slope.
name: A Narrow Crawlspace

desc:
The walls of this passage are covered with sharp and jagged protuberances, as if
the stone was simply ripped out of place. The tunnel slopes upward slightly from
north to south, and the ceiling drops to the south as well. The floor is also
covered with dung - probably from some sort of canine.
name: Rough Passageway

desc:
Savagely hewn from the black rock deep beneath the Trollshaws, the tunnel speaks
of the power of digging trolls. Another passage of some kind - of much finer
make - opens up to the west, while to the south the passageway rises a bit as it
disappears into the darkness.
name: Rough Passageway

desc:
The downward slope of the tunnel has increased! You lose your footing here and
there but manage to maintain your balance. You begin to wonder if the slope of
the tunnel will continue to get steeper, and if it is wise to continue onward
into the darkness.
name: A Steep Tunnel

desc:
Three tunnels come together here in a twisting intersection. The one leading
downward proceeds at a gentle slope and is lined with grey rock. The second
tunnel enters this area from the north, and seems to be cut through heavily
packed earth. The final tunnel, a wide passageway indeed, slopes in from the
ceiling.
name: Intersection

desc:
You are in the old abandoned cellar of the Inn. All around there are the
shattered remains of wine casks and beer barrels. It appears that the end here
was sudden and merciless. There are bones here and there are signs that work was
interrupted unexpectedly. You notice a sign on the wall.
name: An Abandoned Cellar

desc:
The walls here are made from heavily packed earth. It seems that very large
hands dug these tunnels ages ago. You can notice roots and rocks holding
together the walls of this passageway. There is a tunnel leading north, further
into the packed earth and tunnels leading west and south, into the darkness.
name: Rough Passageway

desc:
The passageway continues here, going in a north-south direction. The ceiling is
roughly ten feet high, and the walls are fifteen feet apart. Whomever dug this
passageway had tremendous strength, for you can see rocks and roots torn apart,
embedded in the walls.
name: Rough Passageway

desc:
The passageway continues here, slightly sloping down from south to north. You
can just barely tell there is a slope at all, due to the rough and irregular
nature of the walls, ceiling and floor.
name: Rough Passageway

desc:
The sloping passageway continues on, sloping down from south to north. It's
walls, ceiling and floor are made of rough, packed earth. It appears that these
tunnels were dug out by great hands. The air in here is stale and damp, due to
the lack of sunlight.
name: Rough Passageway

desc:
The walls of the passageway are much rougher here than farther south. It seems
as if the packed earth leads into a rock to the north. This appears to be a
crest in the passage - both north and south it begins to fall. The ceiling is
low as well - the trolls that evidently inhabit this place surely must find it
uncomfortable. There is a predominant stench in the air, making breathing very
difficult.
name: Rough Passageway

desc:
You are in a cave littered with animal and human remains. There appears to be
little distinction between race or species among the littered bones and garbage.
It appears the trolls that live in these tunnels will feast upon anything. A
steep chute leads down further into the darkness, from where guttural sounds can
be heard.
name: Cave

desc:
The grey, stone walls are damp and cold. Moisture seeps in from the ground
above, making the surface of the rocks clammy. The hard dirt floor sucks
greedily at any moisture that may fall off of the rocks. The passageway
continues east and west. A dank smell hovers in the air, like rotting flesh.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
You made a good decision when you brought that extra food. The tunnel here has
stopped sloping downward and is much drier as a result. It continues off into
the darkness to both the east and west.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
A twisting natural tunnel winds through the rock here. To the east you see a
great pile of rubble where the roof has fallen in. A small crawlway leads
downward and to the south.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
A winding, cramped crawlway leads through the stone. Dripping water makes a
monotonous sound from an unknown direction. The crawlway opens up into a larger
tunnel to the north, while to the west it proceeds on its twisting way into the
darkness.
name: A Dark Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel bends slightly to the north, but continues generally east-west. From
the walls large rocks protrude, and the ground is uneven. Rubble and stone-dust
coat the floor, as if the excavation had just taken place.
name: Battered Tunnel

desc:
This ragged tunnel slopes downward. The walls were made with brute force,
lacking in subtlety or skill. Large feet have trodden the ground recently.
name: Battered Tunnel

desc:
This passage is apparently not as frequented as the larger one to the north. The
walls are rough-hewn, as usual, and to the west the passage slopes down.
name: Side Passage

desc:
The passageway dead-ends a short distance to the west in a pile of rubble. To
the east it rises sharply, towards the main portion of the caves. A small side
passage leads down into some sort of a chamber.
name: Side Passage

desc:
This squalid hole is filled with chewed and broken bones. Skulls decorate the
walls and the stench is overpowering.
name: Sleeping Chamber

desc:
A cool breeze flows through this dimly lit cavern. Large stalactites hang from
the tall ceiling, as water drips from their long, cone-shaped tips. A big sloppy
sign has been scrawled on the wall of this cave.
name: A Little Cavern

desc:
The cave about you is filled with rocks and boulders, fallen from the ceiling
and walls. The boulders are less frequent to the west, and to the east an ugly
hole has been bashed into the rock pile.
name: A Large Pile of Rocks

desc:
The foul smells of something that has been unburied fill the entire tunnel. A
small light burns from the east, revealing a little stairway heading down into a
little room.
name: A Smelly Tunnel

desc:
You enter a dark, smelly tunnel. A rank odor of rotting flesh is carried by a
light breeze from somewhere inside the dark entrance.
name: A Smelly Tunnel

desc:
Travel on this little stairway is difficult. Assorted rocks have been thrown
together to make a rough stairway. The light of a torch to the east shows a
little, empty room.
name: A Little, Rocky Stairway

desc:
As you enter this room, the smell of decaying flesh hits you in the face. You
see to the north and south what looks to be piles of dead bodies and stripped
carcasses.
name: A Dank and Putrid Chamber

desc: You are now walking upon piles of dead bodies... Are you sick?
name: The Body Dump

desc:
You can hardly walk through this disgusting, reeking, little cave. Ragged people
beg and claw at you as if there was no hope for life. Rats run rampant over
decaying corpses and litter.
name: Food Storage

desc:
You are in a dark and spartan cave which you recognise as the birthplace of the
Troll race. This recognition is one of racial rather than personal memory, on
the wall is a mural of an imposing dark clothed person standing above a
collection of hideous monsters some of which resemble yourself. Your pitiful
mind struggles to draw meaning from the picture but it fails.
name: Chamber of the Trolls

desc:
To the south the tunnel walls converge, but to the north the way opens out
somewhat, and both the floor and roof climb higher. It should be possible to
swim to the surface in that direction.
name: In the Depths of an Underground Waterway

desc:
The cavern is large and lofty, though the roof falls sharply to the floor at the
southern end, and the floor slopes upward to the east, so that at that end there
is dry land. The rest of the chamber is filled with cold, dark and almost
stagnant water, which has a sharp metallic tang. A sluggish inflow seeps through
a heavy pile of rocks and boulders, which lies against the northern wall, and if
this were once a lively underground stream, it is clear that this obstruction
played at least some part in bringing its waters to an almost complete
standstill. But why they did not simply drain away cannot be told from here.
name: On an Underground Waterway

desc:
This cavern appears to be a natural one, its walls, floor and even its roof worn
quite smooth by the action of water. Sloping downward to the south and to the
west, one end of the cave is covered by the chilly water of a near- stagnant
underground waterway.
name: A Smooth-walled Cavern

desc:
This crude but functional flight of stairs has been crafted in the simplest of
ways: a series of stone slabs of various shapes and sizes has been arranged to
secure footholds along a steeply-angled shaft. The top of the stairs leads to a
large opening, while the lower part veers off in an eastwardly direction. In
contrast to the turning and sloping nature of the stone steps, an almost
horizontal shaft cuts a path southwards through the rock and soil.
name: Crudely-built Staircase

desc:
With nothing supporting this tunnel except the occasional latticework of roots,
there is little to suggest that this is the handiwork of skilled craftsmen. The
walls are part dirt, part bare rock; the rock seems to have been tunneled
through with little care, and bears deep scars from the crude excavation
process. A deep and earthy smell hangs heavily in the air, partially masking a
faint undertone of something far more acrid.
name: A Crude, Earthy Tunnel

desc:
A crude shaft, the walls bearing scars from a hasty excavation process, allows
passage either further into the depths below, or up the slope to the west. The
bare stone floor is etched with deep grooves, presumably the result of large and
heavy objects being dragged or pushed from one place to another.
name: A Dark Shaft

desc:
The earthy tunnel walls give way to bare rock to the north and east. No attempts
appear to have been made to tunnel through the rock; the tunnel instead bends
here, following a path of least resistance through the softer soil. A crevice in
the rock to the north extends from the ceiling of the tunnel all the way to the
floor, widening and deepening as it descends; at the level of the floor it is
large enough to allow access as it continues straight down into the cave system.
name: A Crude, Earthy Tunnel

desc:
A tunnel of varied width snakes its way further through the soil and bedrock as
it continues from south to north. The crude craftsmanship suggested both by the
irregular sizing of the tunnel, and the chunks of broken rock and hacked-up
roots left in piles along the walls, indicates a hasty excavation. The southern
part of the tunnel narrows considerably as it reaches a natural grotto.
name: A Crude, Earthy Tunnel

desc:
A small, natural grotto lies nestled deep within the bedrock and loose soil. The
smooth, aged surface of the rock suggests that it is indeed a naturally
occurring cavern, and not the result of digging. This impression is underscored
by a contrasting, roughly-cut tunnel that has left a gaping hole through the
rock and soil to the north. Debris piles have been left at the junction of
tunnel and grotto, almost as if someone or something broke through the wall with
sheer force.
name: Natural Grotto

desc:
A slightly-slanted tunnel offers passage through the dark, smooth bedrock.
Though the smooth walls of the tunnel suggest a natural origin, someone has made
makeshift steps by hacking away at the floor to provide better footholds. There
is a large opening leading downwards, wide enough to cater to even the largest
of beasts.
name: Natural Passageway

desc:
Debris from a cave-in covers the ground in this partially-obscured passageway.
The tunnel tapers off into a seemingly impassable crawlspace to the east while
the western direction offers a far more conveniently-sized opening.
Partially-eaten remnants of humanoid corpses have been left near the rockfall.
name: A Caved-in Passageway

desc:
The walls of the tunnel here are jagged: the surrounding bedrock is broken and
cracked, leaving deep crevices and protruding edges. The passageway itself is
relatively level; the rock underfoot forms a somewhat concave surface as it
slopes up to form the surrounding walls. Droplets of water have beaded on the
smoother of the surrounding surfaces, and the air is damp and cool.
name: Natural Passageway

desc:
The western end of this tunnel has caved in, leaving a pile of dirt and broken
rocks. The rockfall appears to have originated in a cavern above and broken
through into this passageway; two particularly large slabs of stone have wedged,
leaning against each other, to form an impromptu archway while keeping the
access-way into the cave above clear from debris. As the tunnel continues to the
east, away from the rockfall, the surrounding stone becomes smooth, suggesting
that this tunnel was formed naturally by water running through the bedrock long
ago.
name: Partially-collapsed Passage

desc:
Deep furrows mar floor and walls alike throughout the shaft. Rocks of varying
shape and size are scattered along the passageway; some were clearly broken from
the deep gouges in the walls, while the comparatively lighter colours of others
suggest that they did not originate in this part of the tunnel. A faint, putrid
odour hangs in the air.
name: A Dark Shaft

desc:
Strong odours fill this large cavern. The walls, roughly-cut from the
surrounding stone, suggest that this cave is not a natural feature. Large piles
of refuse, consisting of a mix of debris, faeces, bones and half-rotted pieces
of flesh, have been deposited throughout. A smaller cave has been dug in the
bedrock to the north, while passageways to the south and east grant access to a
nearby network of tunnels.
name: A Refuse-littered Cavern

desc:
Excavation tools, both broken and functional, have been piled on the floor of
this cave. The curious presence of shackles and lengths of chain may indicate
that the cave is used as storage for living creatures as well as inanimate
objects; nothing, however, is currently chained here. A putrid smell hangs heavy
in the air towards the southern end of the cave.
name: Storage Cave

desc:
Walls of rock to the east and west press in close here, creating a vertical
shaft that drops straight down into the depths of the cave system. The walls
appear to be made from great shelves of stone, stacked unevenly upon each other
to form tall but shallow steps and recesses; the resulting ledges are too far
apart to be used ladder-like when ascending or descending, and are mostly too
shallow to be stood upon. One ledge, wider than the rest, is wide enough to
allow purchase; it is precarious, however, slick from condensation and the piles
of bat guano that cover every horizontal surface.
name: Precarious Ledge

desc:
Piles of refuse partially obstruct this passage. Those nearest to the centre of
the tunnel have been trampled down by passing feet; bones, skins, and faecal
matter have been spread from wall to wall, creating a slightly slippery layer
that covers the bedrock beneath. Closer to the walls the piles are less
disturbed; some are heaped nearly to the ceiling, their contents buried beneath
a moving layer of large, iridescent flies. The air here is thick with the odours
of putrefaction and faeces.
name: A Refuse-littered Tunnel

desc:
A wide but low space opens up here at the juncture of several tunnels. The rock
overhead is pitted, hinting at many aborted attempts to enlarge this cavern; the
rubble obstructing the passageways suggests that not all attempts were in vain.
Several distinct paths have been cleared through the piles of dirt and broken
rock, although several large boulders have not been moved from the centre of the
junction, the point at which all paths meet.
name: Subterranean Juncture

desc:
Broken rock fills this passage, creating unstable footing and making the ceiling
appear low. The cracks in the ceiling, paired with several badly-broken sheets
of stone on top of the rubble, suggest that there was a minor collapse here at
some point in the past. The original floor of the tunnel has been buried under
fallen rock, but enough space remains to allow passage from north to east.
name: Rubble-filled Tunnel

desc:
Huge chunks of broken rock fill this part of the tunnel, resulting in a low
ceiling and difficult passage. The rubble is stable; most of the rocks are large
or tightly-packed enough not to shift underfoot, although in spots narrow
crevices extend a significant distance downwards. The rubble is piled highest
along the walls, reaching almost all the way to the ceiling; it appears that the
rubble in the centre of the tunnel has been pushed aside, creating a slightly
more level, and spacious, path.
name: Rubble-filled Tunnel

desc:
Broken rocks are piled in the western part of this passageway, reaching nearly
to the ceiling and creating difficult obstacles over which to pass. A path has
been partially cleared through the centre of the rubble, the rocks pushed to
either side; the path extends into the tunnel to the west, which is similarly
obstructed. In the eastern part of this passage, however, the rubble has been
completely cleared away, revealing a spacious tunnel that slopes downwards.
name: Rubble-filled Tunnel

desc:
A wide burrow of hard-packed dirt runs from north to south. The walls to the
east and west widen considerably before the dirt gives way to solid rock; deep
gouges in the rock suggest that attempts were made to expand this tunnel in both
directions before being abandoned in favour of easier passage through the soil.
The air is cool and damp in this part of the tunnel; it has a strong earthy
scent that carries a hint of mould and decay.
name: Earthy Burrow

desc:
A tunnel winds through the hard-packed dirt here, curving from west to north.
The walls and the ceiling of the tunnel are furrowed, creating ridges that
appear to spiral down the passage. Some of the deeper furrows have exposed an
underlying layer of rock; in places this rock has scratches and gouges,
presumably left over from the excavation process.
name: Earthy Burrow

desc:
Walls of rock to the east and west press in close, creating a natural fissure
that starts here and extends upwards. The bottom of the fissure is covered with
gravel and larger rocks that have broken from the walls; some of the gravel is a
lighter colour than the surrounding rock, suggesting that it has fallen from
much higher up. The western wall is smooth and almost completely vertical, while
the eastern wall is staggered, the stone alternately angled inwards to form
recesses and outwards to form shallow ledges. One of these recesses gives way to
an earthy tunnel that cuts under the rock ledge above; it is wide and tall, and
does not appear to have been formed naturally.
name: Narrow Walls

desc:
The slope in this part of the shaft becomes steep as its descent continues to
the south. The rock that forms the floor of the shaft has been dug out crudely,
creating a tunnel floor that is neither level nor smooth; passage through the
shaft here is made easier by this irregular footing, as the raised chunks of
rock and deep gouges serve as footholds on the steep slope. The air is cool and
slightly damp, with moisture beading on the furrowed rock walls.
name: A Dark Shaft

desc:
Here, the digging activities of the trolls have run into a large, natural
cavern. Tunnel openings in the northeast corner of the cave show where the
trolls burst through the wall as they were burrowing; large, broken stones are
piled near the openings, having been pushed aside but not removed. The ceiling
is high, and the cavern floor is littered with huge, craggy boulders of various
sizes.
name: Expansive, Bouldered Cavern

desc:
A rock wall curves around here from east to south, forming the northwestern
corner of this huge cavern. The ceiling above is cracked, pitted, and uneven; it
is possible that some of the many sharp-angled boulders strewn across the floor
here have fallen from above. The presence of truncated columns in various places
support this possibility; they appear to have been at one time growing
stalagmites, but are now broken, smashed by the same huge rocks that now demand
circuitous pathways when traversing the cavern.
name: Expansive, Bouldered Cavern

desc:
The southern wall in this part of the cavern appears to have caved in at some
point in the past; a large rockfall has spread outwards towards the middle of
the cave, creating an uneven slope that extends from the ceiling down to the
floor. A significant amount of dirt is mixed in with the rubble, and has been
spread across the cave floor; many large footprints can be seen in the dirt,
forming criss-crossing paths that extend in every direction.
name: Expansive, Bouldered Cavern

desc:
An enormous cavern opens up here and extends to the north and east. Giant
boulders and cracked slabs of stone litter the cave floor; some of these are
smooth, but most are badly pitted and eroded. Small spider webs fill the
pockmarks in some of the stones, and larger webs are stretched between the
crevices created where several rocks have been stacked together. Some of these
stacks of stones are irregular and suggestive of rockfall rather than purposeful
construction.
name: Expansive, Bouldered Cavern

desc:
A wide, low grotto has been dug out of the bedrock. The walls are a mix of brown
earth and grey stone, and large holes open where boulders have been removed. The
floor of the cave has been stomped flat and smooth; no rocks are visible in the
dirt underfoot. Crude sleeping piles of straw and fur line one wall, while
several bones and other unidentifiable mounds of waste lie in the opposite
corner.
name: Troll Den

desc:
A tunnel through unstable, crumbling bedrock comes to an end here. A massive
cave-in has blocked the southern part of the passageway, and a hole as wide as
the tunnel itself opens up just before the massive piles of rocks and boulders.
Rocks are scattered across the tunnel floor, and the occasional creak of
boulders scraping against one another deep in the rubble lends little faith to
the idea that this area has completely stabilized after the collapse.
name: Crumbling Passage

desc:
This tunnel is filled with rubble and broken rocks, making the ceiling appear
low. Most of the rocks are large enough not to shift underfoot, although some
are unstable; passage over and through the rubble is made more difficult by the
latter, as sudden shifts can reveal surprisingly deep crevices in the otherwise
tightly-packed debris. The rubble has been cleared from the very southern part
of this tunnel, revealing a large and relatively level passageway; for whatever
reason, the rest of the tunnel has not been cleared in a similar fashion.
name: Rubble-filled Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel is really a natural cave that has been expanded by the brute power
of stony troll fists. The stink of troll sweat fills your head, proving that
many trolls spend a lot of time here. This must be the trolls' home, the place
where they conduct whatever crude business they occupy themselves with when not
crushing prey.
name: Trolls' Home

desc:
This passage is near the surface, judging by the few tree roots that are
visible. Somewhere above lie the dark Trollshaws. The walls are made of packed
earth, hardened by the passage of time. However, any pleasant earthy smell is
overwhelmed by the foetid odour of trolls.
name: Twisted Passageway

desc:
Long ago, in the endless dungeons of his Hell of Iron, Melkor spawned the troll
race. Angband was destroyed in the War of Wrath, swallowed by the sea with the
land of Beleriand. But this black and desolate pit somehow survived, far beneath
the ocean, a last remnant of a once mighty fortress of evil. And in this dark
and foul chasm you find yourself, for a time, confined, unable to participate in
the ongoing wars of Middle-earth. A grubby and tattered sign is posted on the
wall for you to read.
name: In a Subterranean Pit

desc:
Deep in forgotten places, far beneath the ruined halls of Angband, lies this ash
and slag-filled pit. It is no longer a part of the tales of Middle-earth, and
yet some news of those lands still manages somehow to reach you here.
name: A Cramped Corner of the Pit

desc:
This part of the cave is small and even darker than anything else around. In the
middle of the room lies a pool that is so dark it draws even your twisted life
in. It seems to go right to the darkest, most evil places that lie deep down
under Arda, rightly forgotten and full of terrible power.
name: The Pitch-black Pool
